steps.title: Locate the clue about image-sharing's original role
steps.content: The passage calls image-sharing a “secondary feature” that became popular only later, then notes the company shifted to making it the app's centerpiece. This hierarchy—secondary, not primary—is the decisive clue.

steps.title: Match the word to the hierarchy
steps.content:
”Subordinate to” expresses “lower in importance than”—the exact relationship between secondary and primary features. The other choices fail: “duplicated in” and “extracted by” have no logical meaning here, while “intrinsic to” would claim image-sharing was essential to the main purpose, contradicting its secondary status.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
